Pedido.title: Pedido
Pedido.description: Represents a credit order (UCS) entry in the system, serving as the main unit for auditing and traceability. This entity aggregates key information about the order and its overall validation status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pedido (credit order) entity.

orderDate.description: The date when the credit order was placed.

companyName.description: The name of the company placing the credit order.

cnpj.description: The CNPJ (Brazilian company registration number) of the company.

program.description: The name of the program associated with the credit order.

state.description: The Brazilian state (UF) where the credit order originated.

quantity.description: The quantity of credits requested in the order.

value.description: The monetary value of the credit order.

orderHash.description: The existing unique hash for this credit order from the NXT system.

isAudited.description: Indicates whether the credit order has been manually marked as audited.

status.description: The current validation status of the credit order. Determines migration readiness.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the credit order record was initially created in the system.

Movimento.title: Movimento
Movimento.description: Represents a single movement or transaction associated with a credit order, providing granular traceability and validation data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Movimento (movement/transaction) entity.

pedidoId.description: Reference to the Pedido entity this movement belongs to. (Relationship: Pedido 1:N Movimento)

rawData.description: The raw data string associated with the movement, typically provided externally.

movementHash.description: The unique hash for this movement, crucial for global deduplication and integrity checks.

type.description: The type of movement, indicating its origin or nature (e.g., 'government', 'client', 'other').

origin.description: The origin point or entity from which the movement of credits initiated.

destination.description: The destination point or entity to which the movement of credits was directed.

quantity.description: The quantity of credits involved in this specific movement.

isDuplicate.description: Indicates whether this movement's hash has been detected as a duplicate elsewhere in the system.

isValidated.description: Indicates whether this movement has passed all internal validation checks, including non-duplication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the movement record was initially created in the system.

definition.description: Identifies users with auditor privileges. The existence of a document at `/auditors/{request.auth.uid}` grants system-wide read/write access to audit data. This collection ensures Authorization Independence by centralizing global role checks. Documents typically contain `uid`, `email`, and `assignedAt` for traceability, though the mere existence of the document is sufficient for authorization.

params.name: auditorId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who is an auditor.

definition.description: Main collection for credit orders. Each document includes fields such as `orderDate`, `companyName`, `orderHash`, `isAudited`, and `status`. Accessible by authenticated auditors, with authorization determined by the `/auditors` collection.

params.name: pedidoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific credit order (Pedido).

definition.description: Subcollection for individual movements or transactions associated with a specific credit order. Each document contains `pedidoId` for referential integrity back to its parent `Pedido`, along with `movementHash`, `type`, `isDuplicate`, and `isValidated`. Access is granted to authenticated auditors based on their global role, consistent with the parent `Pedido`'s access.

params.name: pedidoId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent credit order (Pedido).

params.name: movimentoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific movement or transaction within a Pedido.

definition.description: Centralized collection to enforce global uniqueness of all `movementHash` values across the system. The document ID is the `movementHash` itself. Each document stores which `pedidoId` and `movimentoId` first registered this hash, and its `createdAt` timestamp. This is critical for the global hash validation system and for enabling secure atomic checks in security rules to prevent duplication.

params.name: movementHash
params.description: The unique hash string of a movement, serving as the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The data structure is meticulously designed to create a secure, scalable, and debuggable Firestore implementation for the LedgerTrust Audit system, directly addressing the reported permission error and adhering to all core design principles and mandates.

## Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):

**1. Global Auditor Role for Authorization:** The system's authorization model is based on a global role for auditors. A dedicated root-level collection, `/auditors/{auditorId}`, is introduced. The mere existence of a document `auditors/{request.auth.uid}` in this collection grants a user system-wide 'auditor' privileges. This completely decouples user authorization from specific `Pedido` or `Movimento` documents.

**Elimination of Hierarchical Dependencies:** This design is paramount for Authorization Independence. Firestore Security Rules for `Pedido` and `Movimento` collections will *not* require `get()` calls to parent documents or other data paths to determine whether a user can access them. Instead, all access decisions will primarily rely on a single, efficient `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/auditors/$(request.auth.uid))` check. This significantly improves performance, enables atomic operations (transactions/batches) without complex `get()` dependencies, and makes security rules far simpler and easier to debug, directly resolving the complexities associated with hierarchical authorization.

## QAPs (Queries as Permissions):

**1. Secure and Efficient Listing:** The reported `FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions ... method: "list", path: "/databases/(default)/documents/pedidos"` is directly resolved by this structure. Since all documents within `/pedidos` and `/pedidos/{pedidoId}/movimentos` share the same security posture (i.e., they are accessible by any authenticated user identified as an 'auditor'), a straightforward Firestore Security Rule checking for the global auditor role (`allow read, list: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/auditors/$(request.auth.uid));`) will securely permit `list` operations. This ensures that rules are not used as filters; rather, they grant broad access to auditors for all relevant audit data, making `list` operations secure, performant, and predictable.

## Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:

**1. Hierarchical Paths for Core Data:** The primary entities, `Pedido` and `Movimento`, are structured using a clear hierarchical path: `/pedidos/{pedidoId}/movimentos/{movimentoId}`. This naturally models the `1:N` relationship where each `Pedido` can have multiple `Movimentos`, aligning with best practices for related data and simplifying data retrieval for a specific order.

**2. Dedicated Global Hash Control Collection:** A new, root-level collection, `/globalMovementHashes/{movementHash}`, is established. This collection serves as the central registry for all unique `movementHash` values across the entire system. By storing each `movementHash` as a document ID, it enables efficient and atomic global uniqueness checks (e.g., using `exists()` in security rules during `Movimento` creation) to fulfill the critical business rule of preventing hash duplication. This segregation cleanly separates the core business entities from the integrity control mechanism, allowing for distinct security postures and optimized operations.

## Data Clarity and Predictability:

**1. Explicit State Modeling:** The `Pedido` entity includes a dedicated `status` field (with states like 'ok', 'pendente', 'erro') and an `isAudited` boolean flag. These fields explicitly model the order's validation and audit status, rather than inferring it from multiple other fields, ensuring clarity for application logic and UI.

**2. Consistent Naming:** Strict semantic naming conventions are applied. Wildcards (`{pedidoId}`, `{movimentoId}`, `{auditorId}`, `{movementHash}`) are descriptive, and all entity field names strictly adhere to the provided `backend.entities` schemas (e.g., `orderDate`, `companyName`, `orderHash` for `Pedido`, and `rawData`, `movementHash` for `Movimento`).

This comprehensive design directly resolves the permission error, ensures a highly secure and scalable architecture, and provides a clear, maintainable foundation for the LedgerTrust Audit system.
